payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Provide task visibility and enable claiming by children
payload.justification: Task claiming is a core strategic function enabling children to register completed work. | Presenting available and completed tasks to children is a strategic visibility function.

payload.description: Define tasks and rewards
payload.justification: Task and reward definition is a key strategic function performed by parents to establish the pocket money system.

payload.description: Display weekly earnings to children
payload.justification: Showing earnings totals at week end is a strategic presentation function for children.

payload.description: Provide earnings and task completion visibility to parents
payload.justification: Presenting child earnings and completed tasks to parents serves the strategic function of enabling payment.

payload.description: Maintain historical task records
payload.justification: Preserving task data over time is a strategic recordkeeping function for longitudinal analysis.

payload.description: Pocket money task with monetary reward
payload.justification: Tasks are core strategic entities referenced across multiple signals as items that can be defined, completed, and claimed. | Monetary rewards associated with tasks represent a distinct strategic entity central to the pocket money system.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Weekly earnings total is a strategic aggregate entity that both children and parents need visibility into.

payload.description: Historical task data
payload.justification: Historical data is identified as a strategic entity to be maintained for longitudinal analysis purposes.

payload.description: Task assignment to child
payload.justification: The relationship between tasks and children is strategic, involving definition by parents and visibility to children.

payload.description: Task completion by child
payload.justification: Task completion represents a strategic relationship state that drives claiming and payment processes.

payload.description: Weekly task and earnings cycle
payload.justification: The week is the strategic time cycle within which tasks are completed, claimed, and earnings are totaled.

payload.description: Week end earnings review
payload.justification: Week end represents a strategic temporal event when children see their total weekly earnings.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Children are identified as strategic actors who claim tasks, view availability, and see their earnings.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Parents are strategic actors who define tasks, set rewards, and view earnings for payment purposes.

payload.description: Provide children visibility into available and completed tasks and enable them to claim completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Signal explicitly expresses this as a core strategic purpose of the system. | Signal articulates transparency of task state as a strategic goal for children.

payload.description: Enable parents to define tasks and monetary rewards
payload.justification: Signal establishes this parental capability as a key strategic system goal.

payload.description: Enable children to see weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Signal defines earnings visibility as a strategic purpose of the system.

payload.description: Provide parents visibility into child earnings and completed tasks for payment
payload.justification: Signal establishes parental oversight for payment purposes as a strategic objective.

payload.description: Maintain historical task data for longitudinal analysis
payload.justification: Signal indicates the strategic requirement to preserve data over time for analytical purposes.

payload.description: Define tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG923 describes the parental function to define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.

payload.description: View available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG925 describes the business capability for child actors to view task entity states.

payload.description: Aggregate weekly earnings
payload.justification: Signal SG926 describes the business capability for temporal aggregation of earned monetary amounts at weekly boundaries.

payload.description: Report child earnings and task completion for disbursement
payload.justification: Signal SG927 describes the business capability providing parent actors with reporting views for disbursement purposes.

payload.description: Claim pocket money
payload.justification: Signal SG922 defines the system's fundamental purpose including a pocket money claiming mechanism.

payload.description: Complete tasks
payload.justification: Signals SG922 and SG924 describe task completion as a core business process in the system.

payload.description: Completed tasks are associated with child entities
payload.justification: Signal SG924 establishes a business rule about the relationship between completed tasks and children.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly cycle boundaries
payload.justification: Signal SG928 defines a business rule governing the temporal lifecycle of task availability.

payload.description: Task with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG923 explicitly describes the business capability to define task entities with monetary value attributes.

payload.description: Child
payload.justification: Signal SG924 establishes the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and child entities, indicating Child is a business entity.

payload.description: Monetary value of task
payload.justification: Signal SG923 explicitly describes monetary value as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Task state (available or completed)
payload.justification: Signal SG925 describes visibility into available and completed task entity states, indicating state is an attribute.

payload.description: Earned monetary amount
payload.justification: Signal SG926 describes temporal aggregation of earned monetary amounts, indicating this is a quantifiable attribute.

payload.description: Task completion relationship between Child and Task
payload.justification: Signal SG924 establishes the conceptual relationship between completed task entities and child entities.

payload.description: Historical task data
payload.justification: Signal SG929 describes the requirement for maintaining historical task data entities beyond the current operational period.

payload.description: Weekly period for earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Signal SG926 describes temporal aggregation occurring at weekly period boundaries.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG928 defines a weekly cycle whereby task availability resets.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Signals SG924 and SG925 describe task completion as a significant business event that changes task state.

payload.description: Parent
payload.justification: Signals SG923 and SG927 describe parent actors with functions to define tasks and view reports.

payload.description: Child
payload.justification: Signals SG922 and SG925 describe child actors as the primary users who complete tasks and view task states.

payload.description: Enable children to complete tasks and claim pocket money
payload.justification: Signal SG922 defines the fundamental business purpose and goal of the system.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained beyond current operational period
payload.justification: Signal SG929 describes a business requirement that constrains the system to maintain historical data.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Household Participation
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining who participates in the household economy system and their respective roles, rights, and responsibilities within the family structure.

payload.name: Task Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to establishing and maintaining accountable work units with associated value recognition, including the creation, assignment, completion tracking, and historical stewardship of household contributions.

payload.name: Earnings Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for transparent calculation, aggregation, and disclosure of financial entitlements earned through participation, ensuring visibility across authorized stakeholders within defined time boundaries.

payload.name: Historical Records Retention
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to preserve longitudinal performance data for accountability, pattern analysis, and institutional memory across participation lifecycles.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and maintaining the inventory of remunerative tasks, their monetary values, availability states, and periodic reset cycles that govern their ongoing availability to participants.

payload.name: Participation Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for recognizing and recording child participation in tasks, establishing completion relationships, and ensuring that completion events are properly attributed to the child participant who performed them.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for aggregating completed task values into earned amounts over defined periods, reporting those earnings for disbursement, and enabling children to claim their accumulated pocket money.

payload.name: Historical Record Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving historical task and completion data beyond the current operational period to meet retention obligations and support longitudinal accountability.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in the household economy.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child participant as a structural entity within the enterprise scope, derived from the Who-column Actor CCI identifying Child user.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants in the household economy.
payload.rationale: Establishes the parent participant as a structural entity within the enterprise scope, derived from the Who-column Actor CCI identifying Parent user.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain accountability for task assignment to children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to maintaining the relationship between tasks and children through assignment, forming the basis of accountability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain accountability for task completion by children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to maintaining the relationship between completed tasks and children, essential for tracking contributions.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide visibility capability, derived from How-column function CCI.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to enable the claiming capability, derived from Why-column goal CCI.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Distinct obligation to provide visibility into historical completed tasks, separate from the claiming capability for available tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks and monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to enable parental governance over task creation and reward definition, combining How-column function and Why-column goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of weekly earnings to children.
payload.rationale: Functional obligation to enable children to see their earnings; directly supports transparency commitment in earnings stewardship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of child earnings to parents.
payload.rationale: Functional obligation to enable parents to view earnings for oversight and payment decisions; ensures stakeholder transparency.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of completed tasks to parents.
payload.rationale: Functional obligation to enable parents to view task completion for oversight; ensures stakeholder transparency.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly cycle for task completion.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint defining the enterprise commitment to weekly time boundaries for task completion stewardship; governs the cadence at which tasks are completed.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly cycle for earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint defining the enterprise commitment to weekly time boundaries for earnings aggregation; governs the cadence at which earnings are aggregated and disclosed.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable review of earnings at week end.
payload.rationale: Functional obligation tied to the week-end event; supports the earnings review capability within the defined time boundary.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical task records for longitudinal analysis.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the How-column obligation to maintain historical task records with the Why-column governance constraint to preserve data for longitudinal analysis. Both CCIs express a single enterprise commitment to retain historical task data for accountability and pattern analysis purposes.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable for historical task data.
payload.rationale: The What-column entity CCI identifies historical task data as a structural entity the enterprise must recognize and govern. At Row 1, this is an enterprise-scope commitment to the existence and governance of this data entity, without implementation detail.

payload.statement: A child shall be recognised as a participant in the household economy.
payload.rationale: Row 2 elaboration of R001: structural recognition of child as participant entity.

payload.statement: A parent shall be recognised as a participant in the household economy.
payload.rationale: Row 2 elaboration of R002: structural recognition of parent as participant entity.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task assignment to children.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business responsibility for maintaining task assignment accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall account for each child's completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business responsibility for tracking completion accountability per child.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance enabling visibility of tasks in available state to child actors.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: Row 2 system affordance enabling visibility of tasks in completed state to child actors.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle for task completion.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business constraint: weekly cycle governs task completion temporal scope.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate earnings over a weekly period.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business process responsibility for aggregating task values into earnings within weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle for earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business constraint: weekly cycle bounds earnings aggregation period.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data beyond the current operational period.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business constraint: governance rule for retention of historical task records.

payload.statement: Task availability shall reset at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Row 2 business rule: task availability state transitions at weekly cycle boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall comply with retention obligations for historical task data.
payload.rationale: Expresses the constraint aspect of accountability - the governance rule that historical data must be retained (Row 2: 'comply with retention obligations') per D008's Why-column constraint.

payload.statement: The Task conceptual entity shall define: attributes: monetary_value money (new), availability_status lifecycle_state (new).

attributes.name: monetary_value
attributes.description: Monetary value associated with the task

attributes.name: availability_status
attributes.description: Current state of the task indicating whether it is available or completed

payload.rationale: Task entity at Row 2 conceptual level defines the core business attributes: monetary value and availability lifecycle state (available/completed).

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks visible.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement: black-box affordance enabling viewing of tasks without specifying implementation mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall make task availability status visible.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement: black-box affordance enabling viewing of task availability state without specifying implementation mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion status visible.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement: black-box affordance enabling viewing of task completion state without specifying implementation mechanism.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task availability reset at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule (Why/How-column): temporal constraint on task lifecycle state governed by weekly cycle.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability state transitions occur aligned to defined weekly boundary timestamps.

payload.statement: The Child conceptual entity shall define: attributes: child_identifier identifier (ref).

attributes.name: child_identifier
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the child participant

payload.rationale: Child entity at Row 2 establishes the conceptual participant who performs tasks; identity refinement from Row 1 scope-level participant concept

payload.statement: The TaskCompletion conceptual entity shall realise the relationship: attributes: completion_timestamp date (new); relationships to: Child (many-to-one), Task (many-to-one).

attributes.name: completion_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the task was completed

payload.rationale: TaskCompletion realises the relationship between Child and Task expressed in CCI-ROW2-C-What-006, establishing the participation accountability link

payload.statement: The business shall associate each completed task with the child entity who performed it.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule (How-column rule CCI) constraining the business to ensure proper attribution of task completion to the performing child participant

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion event.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to record the task completion event (When-column event CCI); functional obligation to maintain the business artefact behind the boundary

payload.statement: The Earnings conceptual entity shall define: attributes: earned_amount money (new).

attributes.name: earned_amount
attributes.description: Monetary amount earned by a child through task completion

payload.rationale: The Earnings entity captures the earned monetary amount as a conceptual business artefact that the business must maintain

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate task values into weekly earnings for each child.
payload.rationale: The business is responsible for consolidating completed task values over the defined weekly period, as expressed by the How/Process CCI coupled with the When/Cycle CCI

payload.statement: The business shall report child earnings for disbursement.
payload.rationale: The business has a responsibility to provide earnings information to enable disbursement decisions.

payload.statement: The business shall report task completion for disbursement.
payload.rationale: The business has a responsibility to provide task completion information to enable disbursement decisions.

payload.statement: The HistoricalTaskData conceptual entity shall introduce: attributes: task_identifier identifier (new), completion_date date (new), task_description name (new), retention_status lifecycle_state (new).

attributes.name: task_identifier
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the historical task record

attributes.name: completion_date
attributes.description: Date when the task was completed

attributes.name: retention_status
attributes.description: Current retention state of the historical record

payload.rationale: The What-column CCI identifies historical task data as a distinct entity that must be preserved; structurally defining it establishes the conceptual data shape for retention obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data beyond the current operational period.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint requiring retention governance; the business is responsible for preserving completed task records to meet longitudinal accountability obligations.

payload.statement: A parent shall define tasks with monetary values.
payload.rationale: How-column Function CCI describing an actor (parent) interacting with the system boundary to define tasks. Actor subject chosen per boundary test: the defining action crosses the system boundary (reaches in). Normative form uses 'shall' with the actor's real action verb 'define'.

payload.statement: A child can claim pocket money for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: How-column Function CCI and Why-column Goal CCI from the same domain (D007 Earnings Settlement) expressing a unified obligation: the child actor's ability to claim earnings. Actor subject chosen per boundary test: claiming is an action crossing the system boundary. The goal CCI (Why-001) motivates and subsumes the process CCI (How-005). Normative form uses 'can' for actor capability.

payload.statement: A child shall complete tasks.
payload.rationale: How-column Process CCI describing an actor (child) action within the participation accountability domain. Actor subject chosen per boundary test: completing tasks is an action crossing the system boundary (child reaches in to mark completion). Normative form uses 'shall' with the actor's real action verb 'complete'.

payload.statement: A child can claim a task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's capability to claim pocket money from completed tasks, realizing the enterprise commitment at the business boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables the child's claim action, complementary to the actor statement.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks with monetary values.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's capability to create tasks and assign monetary rewards, realizing the enterprise commitment at the business boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition with monetary values available to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables parent task definition, complementary to the actor statement.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain tasks with associated monetary values.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to steward the task inventory with monetary values established by parents.

payload.statement: Task is an entity comprising a monetary value and a task state.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion describing the Task entity composition arising from the parent's task definition responsibility.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance to present aggregated earnings to child actors within the weekly period.

payload.statement: A child can view their weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's capability to access their earnings information, complementary to the system affordance.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance to present child earnings information to parent actors for oversight.

payload.statement: A parent can view child earnings.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's capability to access earnings reports, complementary to the system affordance.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance to present task completion status to parent actors.

payload.statement: A parent can view completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's capability to review task completion, complementary to the system affordance.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to settle task values into earned amounts at the weekly cycle boundary.

payload.statement: A child can review their earnings at week end.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's capability to access earnings review at the weekly cycle boundary.

payload.statement: A parent can review child earnings at week end.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's capability to access earnings review at the weekly cycle boundary for oversight and disbursement preparation.

Business capability for temporal aggregation of earned monetary amounts at weekly period boundaries.
Business capability providing parent actors with reporting views of child earnings and task completion for disbursement purposes.
Business rule defining temporal lifecycle whereby task availability resets at weekly cycle boundaries.
Business requirement for maintaining historical task data entities beyond current operational period.
